title: Spokes must only emit hub signals for cross-spoke-generalizable patterns
summary: Signals are for cross-spoke learning. A spoke must not emit a hub signal for patterns that only affect that spoke. Test: does this learning apply to >=1 other spoke? If no, keep it local. If yes, emit signal. Exception: shared framework tool fixes should be hub task lugs, not signals.
rule: Signal = cross-spoke generalization. Local pattern = local record only.
why_it_matters: Spoke-local signals pollute the hub signal queue and waste reviewer time. 4 such signals were cleaned up in S77 after being mis-emitted across S72-S76.
audit_note: Confirmed delivered to hub WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/signal/delivered/ — closed by audit task-track-rebuild-audit-20260527
decision_reason: Already marked delivered. Audit note: Confirmed delivered to hub WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/signal/delivered/ — closed by a
